title: arundarbor pungens
side_effects: Arundarbor pungens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant's leaves or stems can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Additionally, prolonged exposure to its sap or essential oils can lead to respiratory problems, such as asthma or bronchitis. If not handled with care, the plant's extracts can also cause dermatitis, eczema, or other skin conditions in some individuals.
